    apply (EdgeFinite.of_subgraph (h := by
      -- Prove that M ∆ M' is a subset of the edges of G.
      have h₁ : M ⊆ G.E := hM.1
      have h₂ : M' ⊆ G.E := hM'.1
      have h₃ : M ∆ M' ⊆ G.E := by
        -- Use the fact that M and M' are subsets of G.E to show that M ∆ M' is also a subset of G.E.
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hx ⊢
        -- Consider the cases where x is in M and not in M', or in M' and not in M.
        cases hx with
        | inl hx =>
          -- Case: x is in M and not in M'.
          have h₄ : x ∈ M := hx.1
          have h₅ : x ∉ M' := hx.2
          -- Since x is in M and M ⊆ G.E, we have x ∈ G.E.
          have h₆ : x ∈ G.E := h₁ h₄
          exact h₆
        | inr hx =>
          -- Case: x is in M' and not in M.
          have h₄ : x ∈ M' := hx.1
          have h₅ : x ∉ M := hx.2
          -- Since x is in M' and M' ⊆ G.E, we have x ∈ G.E.
          have h₆ : x ∈ G.E := h₂ h₄
          exact h₆
      exact h₃
    ) : EdgeFinite (G ↾ (M ∆ M'))).
  -- Since (G ↾ (M ∆ M')).loopRemove is a subgraph of G ↾ (M ∆ M'), it is also edge-finite.
